pattern: Maintaining a running sum of even elements and adjusting it by only the single changed element per query avoids rescanning the whole array each time.
hints: Recomputing the even sum from scratch after every query is wasteful since only one element changes.
Keep the current sum of even values; a query only affects whether one element contributes to it.
Before applying a query, if the old value was even subtract it from the running sum; apply the delta; then if the new value is even add it back, and record the running sum.

edge_inputs.description: mixed parity flips across four queries
single element turned odd contributes nothing
even becomes odd then even again at same index
negative even values count toward the sum
all odd start so first sums are zero

stress.description: many elements with many queries hitting random indices
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    queries = [[rng.randint(-10000, 10000), rng.randrange(0, n)] for _ in range(q)]
    return [nums, queries]
stress.note: index always valid in range [0, n)
